executive_summary.title: 🔥 Your Commands Have Been Scientifically Roasted 🔥
executive_summary.verdict: Mixed bag of brilliance and bloat. Some commands are lean machines, others are complexity monsters that would make enterprise architects weep.
executive_summary.key_finding: ultrathink-interactive has achieved the remarkable feat of 118.9 complexity score - that's not 'intelligent', that's digital hoarding disorder.
executive_summary.recommendation: Kill ultrathink-interactive with fire. Promote java-rapid-implementation as your new poster child.

token_consumption.savage_assessment: Your average command consumes 3,249 tokens. That's like Claude having to read a short essay just to understand what you want. The worst offender burns through 5,093 tokens - I've seen entire codebases with less verbosity.

complexity_analysis.savage_assessment: With σ=42.53, your complexity scores have more variance than crypto prices. One command scored 118.9 - that's not complexity, that's complexity with anxiety disorder.

efficiency_scores.savage_assessment: ultrathink-interactive has an efficiency score of 0.008. That's like using a Ferrari to move your couch - technically it works, but you've spectacularly missed the point.

weaknesses: 23 decision points for Java code? That's analysis paralysis
java-rapid-implementation.savage_verdict: Solid B+. Claims to be 'rapid' but takes 2,183 tokens to explain how to write Java fast. It's like a speed reading course that comes with a 50-page manual.

evidence.token_efficiency: 2183 tokens / 23.6 complexity = 92.5 tokens per complexity point

ultrathink-pure-xml.savage_verdict: The efficiency champion with a score of 0.125, but 'pure XML' that needs 471 lines? Pure my ass. It's like claiming minimalism while living in a McMansion.

adhd-hyperfocus-guardian.savage_verdict: C+. Needs 3,005 tokens to tell someone with ADHD to take a break. The irony is palpable - it's so complex it would trigger the very ADHD symptoms it's trying to manage.

evidence.pattern_overload: 22 patterns detected - that's not guidance, that's pattern soup

adhd-evening-reflect.savage_verdict: B-. Takes 3,134 tokens to reflect on your day. That's not reflection, that's writing a dissertation about what you had for lunch. Sometimes a simple journal beats a data warehouse.

evidence.reflection_overhead: 3134 tokens for daily reflection = 200+ words of instructions per thought

ultrathink-interactive.savage_verdict: F. CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. This isn't a command, it's a small ERP system disguised as a thinking tool. With 118.9 complexity and 76 functions, you've created the Command Line equivalent of a Rube Goldberg machine. Efficiency score of 0.008 means it's 93% less efficient than doing nothing.

evidence.complexity_explosion: 118.9 complexity score = 4.6x standard deviations above mean
evidence.function_apocalypse: 76 functions in one file violates every principle of software design

distribution_analysis.outlier_identification: ultrathink-interactive is 1.75 standard deviations above mean
distribution_analysis.clustering: Two distinct clusters: reasonable commands (15-26 complexity) vs monster (118.9)

correlation_analysis.lines_vs_maintainability: Strong negative correlation - more lines = harder to maintain
correlation_analysis.functions_vs_usability: Inverse relationship - too many functions kill usability

immediate_actions: DELETE ultrathink-interactive immediately - it's a complexity cancer
Refactor adhd-hyperfocus-guardian - 37.6 complexity for a timer is insulting
Consider promoting java-rapid-implementation as template for new commands
architectural_guidelines: HARD LIMIT: 20 functions maximum per command

savage_insights.biggest_offender: ultrathink-interactive with 118.9 complexity score deserves its own zip code
savage_insights.efficiency_winner: ultrathink-pure-xml proves that simple can work (when it doesn't try to be clever)
savage_insights.hidden_gem: java-rapid-implementation almost gets it right - just needs to lose some weight
savage_insights.pattern_abuse: 22 patterns in one command? That's not architecture, that's pattern addiction
savage_insights.token_waste: Average 3,249 tokens per command could write actual software instead of meta-software

benchmarker_signature.roasted_by: Dr. Savage McBenchmark, PhD in Code Brutality
benchmarker_signature.final_word: Some of these commands are like using a nuclear reactor to power a nightlight. Effective? Sure. Appropriate? Absolutely not.
